title: zieria smithii var. macrophylla
intro: Zieria smithii var. macrophylla, also known as the 'large-leaved Zieria' or 'native pepperbush', is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the Rutaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, its leaves possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Zieria smithii var. macrophylla are often infused in tea or used in topical creams to harness its medicinal benefits, providing relief from skin irritations and promoting overall skin health.
